title: dactylorhiza maculata subsp. kolaensis
intro: Dactylorhiza maculata subsp. kolaensis, also known as the Kola spotted orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to the mountainous regions of India. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, making it useful in treating wounds, burns, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza maculata subsp. kolaensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate symptoms of fever and cough, and promote tissue healing. The herbal preparation of this plant typically involves drying and crushing the leaves and roots, which can then be used as a powder or decoction in traditional medicine.
